contact.restrictions: Priority is given to smaller public charities located in the San Francisco Bay Area, including Sacramento, California. Education and human services are favored. The foregoing is not intended to be a complete list of priorities. Written solicitations only. The Foundation has no paid staff and does not acknowledge the receipt of applications. Successful applicants will be notified by mail, which mail will include a check for the amount of the grant authorized by the Foundation. Grants generally do not exceed $5,000.
contact.how_to_apply: A simple statement of the purpose of the proposed grant-how it will be used, what project will be supported, etc. Required application materials: 1. IRS tax exemption determination letter. The charity must be recognized as a public charity-not a private foundation. 2. The charity's employer identification number. 3. The charity's most recent financial statements showing: a. A statement of the charity's revenue and expense, showing the charity's sources of support and expenditures for its most recent year. Expense categories should include compensation of management and interest paid on loans secured by real property, if any. b. The charity's balance sheet. 4. The charity's budget for revenue and expense for the current year and, if available, the charity's budget for revenue and expense for the coming year. 5. The average percentage of donations to the charity used for fund raising during the last 2 years.

contact.how_to_apply: The Ram and Lakshmi Sundaram Foundation provides grants to organizations that satisfy the mission of the FoundationTo provide resources and support to improve the lives of culturally, socially and economically disadvantaged persons. It is the responsibility of the Foundation to identify and determine those groups that effectively meet the mission of the Foundation. Contribution requests that will be considered by the Foundation include the following categories: Requests for funds for general operating support or program related supportRequests for sponsorship of functions or events in support of non-profit organizationsRequests for grants will be reviewed by the Foundation. contact.restrictions: the funds must be used in a manner consistent with the mission.
contact.how_to_apply: A letter of intent is to be submitted initially. The letter is to not exceed three pages in length. The letter should describe (1) the person or entity seeking funding, (2) the specific purpose for which funds are sought, (3) the amount requested for one year, including the specific amount sought from the foundation, (4) a general statement about what the proposed program will achieve and how it compares with the mission of the foundation, (5) the anticipated length of time required to be successful in the endeavor sought to be financed, and (6) a list of other funding sources, if any, and whether confirmed or not confirmed. If the initial letter of intent is accepted, applicants must then complete a grant application. The grant application requests the legal name, year founded, current operating budget, date of project, amount requested, total project cost, and geographic area served.

THE FOUNDATION HAS ESTABLISHED POLICIES (ATTACHED) TO GUIDE ITS BOARD OF DIRECTORS IN ALLOCATING ITS DONATIONS. contact.how_to_apply: The Guenther Foundation accepts applications for grants from qualified tax-exempt charitable organizations operating within the State of California. Applications are not accepted from individuals. Regular meetings of the Guenther Foundation Board of Directors are held semi-annually in the winter and summer to consider and take final action on pending applications for grants. Effective with the October 31, 2021 deadline, all applications must be submitted online; the Guenther Foundation will no longer accept mailed or hand-delivered paper copies. Applications must be fully completed directly on the henry L. Guenther Foundation website by October 31 for consideration at the board of directors meeting held in January, and by May 31 for consideration at the meeting held in July. Prospective applicants are requested to communicate with the Guenther Foundation office only by letter or email (no telephone calls please). Please visit guentherfoundation.org/how-to-apply/ for more information.

comparable_grant.purpose: 78771 EXPLORING EQUITABLE ENTERPRISES TO PROMOTE OPPORTUNITY, WEALTH, SECURITY, AND HEALTH FOR DIVERSE WORKERS AND THEIR FAMILIES

contact.deadlines: NO DEADLINES, EXCEPT WHEN FOUNDATION ISSUES A CALL FOR PROPOSAL (CFP) OR REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL (RFP).
contact.restrictions: THE ROBERT WOOD JOHNSON FOUNDATION IS DEDICATED TO TAKING BOLD LEAPS TO TRANSFORM HEALTH IN OUR LIFETIME AND PAVE THE WAY TOGETHER TO A FUTURE WHERE HEALTH IS NO LONGER A PRIVILEDGE, BUT A RIGHT. FOR FIVE DECADES, OUR GRANTMAKING HAS FOCUSED ON DEVELOPING AND PROMOTING RESEARCH, POLICIES, AND INNOVATIONS IN HEALTH AND HEALTH CARE FROM DEVELOPING THE 911 CALL SYSTEM, TO DECREASING SMOKING RATES, TO IMPROVING CARE AT THE END OF LIFE, TO CONFRONTING CHILDHOOD OBESITY, TO INCREASING ACCESS TO HEALTH CARE, TO CULTIVATING LEADERS. OUR GRANTMAKING TAKES A COMPREHENSIVE APPROACH TO HEALTH, ONE ROOTED IN EQUITY AND FOCUSED ON ADDRESSING BARRIERS TO HEALTH CAUSED BY THE INTERSECTION OF STRUCTURAL RACISM, OTHER FORMS OF DISCRIMINATION AND SOCIAL CONDITIONS THAT IMPACT HEALTH. WE DO THIS BY SUPPORTING RESEARCH, PROGRAMS, POLICIES, PRACTICES, SYSTEMS CHANGE, AND NARRATIVE CHANGE AIMED AT SHIFTING THE NATIONAL CONVERSATION ABOUT HEALTH AND HEALTH EQUITY AND BRINGING ABOUT MEANINGFUL CHANGE.
contact.how_to_apply: MOST OF RWJF'S GRANTS ARE MADE THROUGH TARGETED GRANT PROGRAMS AND FUNDING PARTNERSHIPS. RWJF'S FUNDING PRIORITIES AND CALLS FOR PROPOSALS ARE DESCRIBED ON WWW.RWJF.ORG.
ONLY ONLINE APPLICATIONS WILL BE ACCEPTED FOR GRANT CONSIDERATION

comparable_grant.purpose: TO SUPPORT THE WORK AS A JOSIAH MACY JR. FOUNDATION, MACY FACULTY SCHOLAR

contact.restrictions: THE JOSIAH MACY JR. FOUNDATION IS A PRIVATE FOUNDATION WHICH PROVIDES GRANTS TO COLLEGES, UNIVERSITIES, AND OTHER PROFESSIONAL ASSOCIATIONS. THE FOUNDATION'S GRANTS HAVE SUPPORTED PROJECTS THAT BROADEN AND IMPROVE HEALTH PROFESSIONAL EDUCATION. CURRENTLY, IT IS TOTALLY DEVOTED TO IMPROVING HEALTH BY ADVANCING THE EDUCATION AND TRAINING OF HEALTH PROFESSIONALS, AND IT IS THE ONLY NATIONAL FOUNDATION SOLELY DEDICATED TO THIS MISSION.
contact.how_to_apply: ALL APPLICATIONS MUST BE COMPLETED AND SUBMITTED ONLINE AT WWW.MACYFOUNDATION.ORG. ALL APPLICATIONS START WITH A LETTER OF INQUIRY WHICH CONTAINS: -NAMES AND QUALIFICATIONS OF THOSE WHO WOULD CARRY OUT THE PROJECT -BRIEF DESCRIPTION OF THE PROJECT GOALS AND DESIGN, AND HOW IT FITS WITHIN YOUR ORGANIZATION -ESTIMATED PROJECT BUDGET LETTERS OF INQUIRY ARE EVALUATED ON THESE KEY DIMENSIONS: -RELEVANCE TO OUR PRIORITIES; -IMPORTANCE AND ORIGINALITY. -SIGNIFICANCE AND GENERALIZABILITY; AND SUSTAINABILITY.
